messages.text: They're holding the severance hostage behind the NDA. Sign away the right to ever talk about it and they'll pay me to disappear. 'Per my last email,' I declined. Megan doesn't know I declined. I'll tell her this weekend.

Quick one — once we file the OCR complaint, what's the realistic window before HHS even assigns it? I keep reading enforcement takes 12–18 months. Trying to set expectations for myself (and Megan). Not pushing, just mapping the timeline.
You free for the usual call? Need to walk through some opsec stuff and honestly just need to talk it out before I spiral. 9:30 like always.

Been thinking about not waiting on Dana's timeline. Every day I sit on this, the breach notification gap matters less to the people it hit. I know what you'll say. Just thinking out loud. Don't tell Mom I said it.

Sorry for the hour. Hypothetically — if a preservation letter showed up before we file, does that change anything about what I'm allowed to still have in my possession? Asking so I'm not caught flat-footed. Can wait till morning for the real answer.

Patrick. I'm gonna say it plain because nobody else will. You're not okay. 4 hours of sleep, the weight, the 1am texts. I'm with you on the why. But you're making the biggest call of your life at the exact hours you should be asleep. Promise me nothing goes out tonight.

Was gonna FaceTime over coffee but the kids are up and Lila keeps asking why I'm home. Can't do this one with her in the room. Tonight instead? Tell Mom I'm fine even though you already told her I'm not.

You asked again if this is going to be okay and I keep not answering. Let's actually talk tonight after they're down — really talk, not the dishes-and-half-sentences version. There's more in those files than I've said. I should have told you sooner.

Picking up in 2 — give me a sec to put the phone in the kitchen. Bad day. Dana wants to wait on the OCR thing and I'm sitting here watching the clock. Just need to hear a voice that isn't billing me $450/hr.

It's not that I don't trust Dana. It's that her timeline assumes nobody else moves first. What if I just quietly run her plan by one other lawyer? Not to replace her. Just a sanity check. Don't tell Mom you heard me say that.

Hi — saw your profile re: employment/whistleblower matters. Looking for a confidential 15-min consult, this week if possible. Already have counsel; want a second read on sequencing an OCR complaint vs. other disclosure. Can pay the consult fee. Prefer Signal if you have it.
You sound worse than Tuesday, man. Are you actually sleeping? Megan says you were up again. I'm not getting on you, I just — call it 20 tonight, I got an early shift. Drink some water. Eat something.

I have to tell you something and I need you to not freak out. Last night, like 1am, I had the message open. The one to the reporter. Finger was on send. I closed the laptop and walked away but it was right there. That's the closest I've gotten. I don't know what stopped me, honestly.

To be clear on today's call: no journalist contact, none, until OCR and the SEC tip have run their course. I know it feels slow. Slow is what keeps this a whistleblower case and not a CFAA case. Let the record show I advised you. We talk again Thursday.
Confirmed for Monday 4:15pm, 15 min, $75 consult. I'll send a Signal link Sunday. Bring a one-paragraph summary of the timeline and what your current counsel has advised. We'll keep it high-level.
We need to actually talk this weekend. Not the kids-asleep five minutes. A real one. I've been keeping some of this from you and that's not fair to you. Saturday after Lila's down?

(after we talked) Thank you for not blowing up. I meant what I said — there are more documents than I let on. I'm not going to do anything stupid with them, I promise you that. I'll keep you in the loop from here. I love you. Go to bed, I'll lock up.
Running an errand downtown this morning, couple hours. I'll be back in time for Lila pickup, promise. Love you.

[Signal call ~22 min] Vented about Dana again — she wants OCR and the SEC tip to run before anything else. 'Conservative' she keeps saying. I get it, I do. Just feels like watching the building burn while we file the permit. Anyway. Didn't get into the rest of my day. Talk tomorrow.
How's the back holding up after that double? You ice it. I've got dinner — thinking that lemon chicken the kids actually eat.
better, thx. babe we have to figure out COBRA, the deadline is creeping. can we talk tonight?
Realistically — how long before OCR actually moves on a complaint like this? Weeks? Months? I'm not pushing, just need to know what window we're really in.
[Signal call] You sound flat tonight, man. Like running-on-fumes flat.
[same call] Yeah. Honestly haven't really slept since the 4th. Few hours, then I'm up doing the loop at 2am. Watch says 4 and a half. Megan doesn't know how bad. Don't tell Mom that part.
Caleb's club fees are due Friday. $1,400. I know.
we can't just not pay it patrick, he'll be the only kid off the roster. where is it coming from
I'll move it from savings. We'll figure the rest out. I'm sorry — I'm on it.

One thing keeping me up — if MedTrust were to find out I retained a representative sample of files, what's the realistic preservation-letter exposure? Hypothetically. Want to understand the downside before OCR drags.

[Signal call] Someone posted on one of the regulatory forums — sounds like ex-MedTrust, talking about the same breach timeline. Vague but it's the same story. If they go public first I lose the whole frame, Kev.
[same call] Hey. Don't do anything tonight. You hear me? Nothing tonight. Sleep on it, we talk tomorrow when you're not running on no sleep at 9pm.
We need to decide COBRA today, for real. I priced it — it's brutal but Lila's inhaler refills alone make it non-negotiable. Let's just commit at lunch. No more circling back.
What happens if someone else goes first? There's chatter that another former employee might. Does that change our posture at all?
It doesn't change my advice. We hold. Another person surfacing actually strengthens a coordinated record — it does not require you to react. Patience here is protection, Patrick.
[Signal call] Calmer tonight. We pulled the trigger on COBRA, so at least the kids' coverage is settled. One thing off the board feels like a lot right now. Still chewing on the rest but — better.
[FaceTime, family Sunday] Kids said hi to Uncle Kev — Caleb wanted to show you the new cleats. Megan made it onto the call between shifts. Good to just be normal for half an hour. (no other talk)
Better day. COBRA's done, kids are good, slept maybe 6. Talk tomorrow.
Hey. Megan's down with Lila so I can actually talk. You free? Need to not be in my own head for an hour.

Patrick — reaffirming what we discussed. We hold for the full 30 days. Let OCR and the SEC tip work first. No journalist contact, no exceptions. I know it's hard to sit still. Sitting still is the strategy. Call me if the wait gets loud.

Got Dana's portal note again. 'Sit still.' Per my last 30 days, apparently. She's not wrong, I just — every week I wait is a week they get to build the file first. You'd think the careful one would catch a break for being careful.

I hear you. But not tonight, Pat. It's almost midnight and you've got that voice. Promise me — nothing leaves your hands tonight. Sleep first. We talk tomorrow when it's daylight. Don't do anything tonight.
WakeMed HR confirmed the family plan starts the 1st — kids stay covered, no gap. Lila's inhaler refill is in there too. So that's one thing we don't have to be scared about. Love you. We're okay.

Caleb's spring soccer is $1,400 and I just stared at the email for ten minutes. Funny — they offered me money to go away and I said no on principle, and now I'm rationing my kid's cleats. Principle's expensive. Don't tell Mom that part.

Pat — what's actually in the folder you keep working on at night? You said emails. The way you close the laptop when I walk in doesn't read like emails. I'm not mad. I just can't help carry something I'm not allowed to see.

Good seeing your face, son. You looked thin. Whatever's going on at that company — you don't owe those people anything, you hear me? Take care of those babies and your wife. The rest sorts itself. Patricia says call her.

Talked to Dad this morning. Kept it light, he doesn't need the weight. Weirdly steadier tonight though. Megan asked me straight what's in the folder today and I didn't lie great but I didn't add to the pile either. Small win. Going to actually sleep.
Honestly? Fine. Dana's just being thorough, says these things move slow. I'm fine, Meg — just tired. Didn't sleep again. I'll start dinner, don't worry about me.

I know the COBRA letter is sitting there. I'll deal with it tomorrow, I promise. We're not underwater yet — Caleb's soccer can wait a check. And I'm not hiding anything from you, it's just legal stuff I can't fully repeat per Dana. Can we do this when the kids are down? Not here in the kitchen at dinnertime. Please.

calendar.summary: Focus block — no devices (move ThinkPad offline, phone on charger)
COBRA DECLINE — go on Megan's plan (deadline today)
Family game night — phones in the basket
Family board game night — phones in kitchen
